title: smyrnium trifoliatum
intro: Smyrnium trifoliatum, also known as Wild Carrot or Carrot Weed, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its umbrella-like inflorescence and umbrella-like leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. Smyrnium trifoliatum has been used to treat a range of health issues, including indigestion, bloating, and coughs, due to its carminative and expectorant properties. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
